generatedSentence: Our junior worker, often lacked respect among the dukes of the skyscrapers, watched autumn leafs drift. He felt grim, planning to unfollow the rogue hackers using the company backend. This was his test to vindicate himself, studying the wiki for a crucial lesson. His urge to expose their crafty schemes grew, despite the noway attitude of his peers. He needed a clear mark to make an impact. The electro-dynamo hummed, a constant motion. The venomous hackers, with alarming speed, had blacked out critical data. This incident left many dejected. Their ultimatum was clear: they’d even tried to kidnap a jeweler, making the dock a dangerous shipway. He felt trapped, numb with the sheer obscurity of their digital wastes. These fanatics were maneaters of the system, their alchemy turning good data rotten. Hurray, he thought, a friendly dwarf named Turner joined him. Wearing a rustproof waistcoat, he'd untick every corrupted file. The sun was nearly setting, casting long lines on the glass. He knew the goats of the backend had crusted the foundation with bad code, like saccharin on cheese. His undying resolve, sharpened by a seminar on cyber warfare, gave him the bullet-like focus to express his findings, despite imperfect data. A swift whirls of jets flew oversea. His earning from this immense effort brought relief, not just for his family, his baby twins, but for everyone. The writers celebrated him, sending faraway vouchers. He laughed, knowing he’d overcome the illness of the system. His slim, rustproof order and an inkhorn full of new ideas were his crown. No more dryads of data, no more wasted resources, just pure, clean digital harvests. His teacup, once filled with acids, now held the promise of a brighter, non-grouchy future. He watched the letterbox, a new era dawning.
panelSentences: Our junior worker, often lacked respect among the dukes of the skyscrapers, watched autumn leafs drift. He felt grim, planning to unfollow the rogue hackers using the company backend. This was his test to vindicate himself, studying the wiki for a crucial lesson. His urge to expose their crafty schemes grew, despite the noway attitude of his peers. He needed a clear mark to make an impact.

The electro-dynamo hummed, a constant motion. The venomous hackers, with alarming speed, had blacked out critical data. This incident left many dejected. Their ultimatum was clear: they’d even tried to kidnap a jeweler, making the dock a dangerous shipway. He felt trapped, numb with the sheer obscurity of their digital wastes. These fanatics were maneaters of the system, their alchemy turning good data rotten.

Hurray, he thought, a friendly dwarf named Turner joined him. Wearing a rustproof waistcoat, he'd untick every corrupted file. The sun was nearly setting, casting long lines on the glass. He knew the goats of the backend had crusted the foundation with bad code, like saccharin on cheese. His undying resolve, sharpened by a seminar on cyber warfare, gave him the bullet-like focus to express his findings, despite imperfect data. A swift whirls of jets flew oversea.

His earning from this immense effort brought relief, not just for his family, his baby twins, but for everyone. The writers celebrated him, sending faraway vouchers. He laughed, knowing he’d overcome the illness of the system. His slim, rustproof order and an inkhorn full of new ideas were his crown. No more dryads of data, no more wasted resources, just pure, clean digital harvests. His teacup, once filled with acids, now held the promise of a brighter, non-grouchy future. He watched the letterbox, a new era dawning.
rawPanelSentences: Our junior worker, often lacked respect among the dukes of the skyscrapers, watched autumn leafs drift. He felt grim, planning to unfollow the rogue hackers using the company backend. This was his test to vindicate himself, studying the wiki for a crucial lesson. His urge to expose their crafty schemes grew, despite the noway attitude of his peers. He needed a clear mark to make an impact.

The electro-dynamo hummed, a constant motion. The venomous hackers, with alarming speed, had blacked out critical data. This incident left many dejected. Their ultimatum was clear: they’d even tried to kidnap a jeweler, making the dock a dangerous shipway. He felt trapped, numb with the sheer obscurity of their digital wastes. These fanatics were maneaters of the system, their alchemy turning good data rotten.

Hurray, he thought, a friendly dwarf named Turner joined him. Wearing a rustproof waistcoat, he'd untick every corrupted file. The sun was nearly setting, casting long lines on the glass. He knew the goats of the backend had crusted the foundation with bad code, like saccharin on cheese. His undying resolve, sharpened by a seminar on cyber warfare, gave him the bullet-like focus to express his findings, despite imperfect data. A swift whirls of jets flew oversea.

His earning from this immense effort brought relief, not just for his family, his baby twins, but for everyone. The writers celebrated him, sending faraway vouchers. He laughed, knowing he’d overcome the illness of the system. His slim, rustproof order and an inkhorn full of new ideas were his crown. No more dryads of data, no more wasted resources, just pure, clean digital harvests. His teacup, once filled with acids, now held the promise of a brighter, non-grouchy future. He watched the letterbox, a new era dawning.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist is a slim figure wearing a streamlined, dark grey tech-suit with glowing blue accents and a determined expression. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist, seen from a low-angle looking up at towering, futuristic skyscrapers, a single autumn leaf falling past his focused face, with a subtle glow of a distant 'wiki' screen reflected in his eyes. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist, in a dynamic medium shot, surrounded by swirling lines of digital code, reaching out as if to untick a corrupted interface, with a menacing, shadowy 'maneater' hacker figure in the background. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist and a short, stout, friendly dwarf character with a mechanical arm, working together on a complex holographic interface, with a subtle sun setting behind them through a glass wall. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist, a wide-angle shot, standing triumphantly on a clean, rustproof digital dock, holding a glowing digital 'crown' aloft, a beautiful cityscape in the background, a small teacup on a pristine surface nearby, and a celebratory letterbox opening.

definitions.definition: An attitude of consideration or high regard

(always plural) Polite greetings, often offered as condolences after a death.

definitions.definition: A particular aspect, feature or detail of something
definitions.example: This year's model is superior to last year's in several respects.

definitions.definition: To abide by an agreement.

A challenge, trial.

A piece of music for this dance (or in triple time).
A simple task.
To dance the waltz (with).

definitions.definition: (in compounds) Compatible with, or not damaging to (the compounded noun).

A hose or pipe, of any size.

definitions.definition: Direction, path.

A letter, a written form of communication.

A more-or-less straight sequence of people, objects, etc., either arranged as a queue or column and often waiting to be processed or dealt with, or arranged abreast of one another in a row (and contrasted with a column), as in a military formation.

To measure.

The cardinality, or number of elements in a set, group, or other structure regardable as a set.

A navigable canal.
Distant.
definitions.example: She lived in a faraway village in a faraway land.

definitions.definition: Not mentally present, as when daydreaming.
definitions.example: There was a faraway look on his face.

definitions.definition: A piece of paper that entitles the holder to a discount, or that can be exchanged for goods and services.
A receipt.
One who or that which vouches.
A copy of a published advertisement sent by the agency to the client as proof of publication.
A mechanical device used in shops for automatically registering the amount of money drawn.
To establish the authenticity of; to vouch for.
To provide a vouch for (an expenditure).
To provide (a beneficiary) with a voucher.
A small portable container, often made of horn, used to carry ink.
(used attributively, of vocabulary) Pedantic, obscurely scholarly.
A mode of transportation, often a train, that travels quickly or directly.
definitions.example: I took the express into town.

definitions.definition: A service that allows mail or money to be sent rapidly from one destination to another.
An express rifle.
A clear image or representation; an expression; a plain declaration.
A messenger sent on a special errand; a courier.
An express office.
That which is sent by an express messenger or message.
Moving or operating quickly, as a train not making local stops.
Specific or precise; directly and distinctly stated; not merely implied.
definitions.example: I gave him express instructions not to begin until I arrived, but he ignored me.

definitions.definition: Truly depicted; exactly resembling.
definitions.example: In my eyes it bore a livelier image of the spirit, it seemed more express and single, than the imperfect and divided countenance.

definitions.definition: (retail) Providing a more limited but presumably faster service than a full or complete dealer of the same kind or type.
definitions.example: Some Wal-Mart stores will include a McDonald's Express.

definitions.definition: A projectile, usually of metal, shot from a gun at high speed.
An entire round of unfired ammunition for a firearm, including the projectile, the cartridge casing, the propellant charge, etc.
Ammunition for a sling or slingshot which has been manufactured for such use.
A printed symbol in the form of a solid circle, (•), often used in lieu of numbers for marking items in a list. (see also bulleted)
A large scheduled repayment of the principal of a loan; a balloon payment.
A rejection letter, as for employment, admission to a school or a competition.
definitions.example: John's not going to any of his top schools; he got a bullet from the last of them yesterday.

definitions.definition: One year of prison time
An ace (the playing card).
Anything that is projected extremely fast.
(in attributive use) Very fast (speedy).
definitions.example: bullet chess

definitions.definition: A plumb or sinker.
(particularly in Northern Ireland) The heavy projectile thrown in a game of road bowling.
A roughly bullet-shaped sweet consisting of a cylinder of liquorice covered in chocolate.
A small ball.
A cannonball.
The fetlock of a horse.
To draw attention to (text) by, or as if by, placing a graphic bullet in front of it.
To speed, like a bullet.
definitions.example: Their debut started slow, but bulleted to number six in its fourth week.

definitions.definition: To make a shot, especially with great speed.
definitions.example: He bulleted a header for his first score of the season.

definitions.definition: To make black; to blacken.
To apply blacking to (something).
To boycott, usually as part of an industrial dispute.
A person who is zealously enthusiastic for some cause, especially in religion.
The striking of one body against another; collision.
The force or energy of a collision of two objects.
definitions.example: The hatchet cut the wood on impact.

definitions.definition: A forced impinging.
definitions.example: His spine had an impingement; L4 and L5 made impact, which caused numbness in his leg.

definitions.definition: A significant or strong influence; an effect.
definitions.example: His friend's opinion had an impact on his decision.

definitions.definition: To collide or strike, the act of impinging.
definitions.example: When the hammer impacts the nail, it bends.

definitions.definition: To compress; to compact; to press into something or pack together.
definitions.example: The footprints of birds do not impact the soil in the way those of dinosaurs do.

definitions.definition: To influence; to affect; to have an impact on.
definitions.example: I can make the changes, but it will impact the schedule.

definitions.definition: To stamp or impress onto something.
definitions.example: Ideas impacted on the mind.

definitions.definition: A star, especially when seen as the centre of any single solar system.
The light and warmth which is received from the sun.
Something like the sun in brightness or splendor.
Sunrise or sunset.
The nineteenth trump/major arcana card of the Tarot.
The thirty-first Lenormand card.
To expose to the warmth and radiation of the sun.
definitions.example: Beautiful bodies lying on the beach, sunning their bronzed limbs.

definitions.definition: To warm or dry in the sunshine.
To be exposed to the sun.
To expose the eyes to the sun as part of the Bates method.
The star that the Earth revolves around and from which it receives light and warmth.
A type of cigarette substantially longer and thinner than normal cigarettes.
definitions.example: I only smoke slims.

definitions.definition: A potato farl.
AIDS, or the chronic wasting associated with its later stages.
Cocaine.
To lose weight in order to achieve slimness.
To make slimmer; to reduce in size.
Slender, thin.
(of something abstract like a chance or margin) Very small, tiny.
definitions.example: I'm afraid your chances are quite slim.

definitions.definition: (rural) Bad, of questionable quality; not strongly built, flimsy.
definitions.example: A slimly-shod lad;

definitions.definition: Sly, crafty.
(originally student slang) Irritable; easily upset; angry; tending to complain.
definitions.example: His boss gets grouchy when deadlines draw near.

definitions.definition: Having the nature of kitsch.
To cause to become numb (physically or emotionally).
definitions.example: The dentist gave me novocaine to numb my tooth before drilling, thank goodness.

definitions.definition: To cause (a feeling) to be less intense.
definitions.example: He turned to alcohol to numb his pain.

definitions.definition: To cause (the mind, faculties, etc.) to be less acute.
To become numb (especially physically).
Physically unable to feel, not having the power of sensation.
definitions.example: fingers numb with cold

definitions.definition: Emotionally unable to feel or respond in a normal way.
definitions.example: numb with shock; numb with boredom

definitions.definition: Causing numbness.
A collection point for mail intended for onward delivery, a secure box or receptacle for this purpose.
A delivery point for mail, a box, compartment or slot for this purpose.
A hidden container that holds a logbook and rubber stamp, found by following clues.
To transfer a widescreen motion picture to home video formats while preserving the original aspect ratio, with the placing of black bars above and below the picture area.
To hunt for letterboxes (containers with logbook and rubber stamp) by following clues.
Transferred to home video formats while preserving the original aspect ratio, having black bars above and below the picture area.

To show mirth, satisfaction, or derision, by peculiar movement of the muscles of the face, particularly of the mouth, causing a lighting up of the face and eyes, and usually accompanied by the emission of explosive or chuckling sounds from the chest and throat; to indulge in laughter.
definitions.example: There were many laughing children running on the school grounds.

definitions.definition: To be or appear cheerful, pleasant, mirthful, lively, or brilliant; to sparkle; to sport.
(followed by "at") To make an object of laughter or ridicule; to make fun of; to deride; to mock.
definitions.example: Don't laugh at my new hat, man!

definitions.definition: To affect or influence by means of laughter or ridicule.
To express by, or utter with, laughter.
